title: Chapter Seventy-Six: The Notice
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Zhu Lianting turned and walked away.

The Ma family warriors followed. The elders who had watched from the edges turned their backs too, silent as stone. Such was the dignity of a prestigious house -- they did not linger over frivolous matters. Intent had been conveyed. That was enough.

Hyeon Won-chang bowed his head slightly beside him, restraining the urge to follow. Chasing now would only make the ruse look clumsy.

*Half a success.*

The sound transmission came quick.

*How is it, Young Hero Jeong? They said they'll accept your intention--*

*Being struck by her palm technique is enough. Many masters saw it.*

Yeon-shin gently pushed away Won-chang's steadying hand and rose. The blow from Zhu Lianting's palm had rattled through him like a temple bell, but his meridians were intact. He had dispersed the force exactly as planned -- redirected its path through his body the way a river parts around a stone.

Unfamiliar warriors approached. Mixed blue and white ranks. Masters from other martial powers who had observed the incident, drifting closer with the careful interest of men who had seen something worth remembering.

"Are you alright?"

"Though we cannot comment on family matters, if you studied in the Ma family, this behaviour is unacceptable."

"Your movement technique was impressive."

They came, examined him briefly, and left. Family matters. No one wanted entanglement with those in power.

One remained. A middle-aged man with blue robe sleeves extending from clasped hands and eyes that drooped in an expression of permanent, amiable calm.

"First time seeing the rumoured rising talent," he said. "Because the main fortress is vast. Jin Ju-rim of Obeying Heaven Wing."

Yeon-shin clasped his hands in return. "Radiant Demon Wing, Lightning Genius."

"Lightning Genius Jeong Yeon-shin." Jin Ju-rim's smile widened. "I heard well from Hwa-sin. Since before you were promoted to blue rank."

Something stirred in memory. Distant. Hwa-sin.

"You mean White Qilin," Yeon-shin said. "Namgung Hwa-sin."

"The same."

He remembered now. The man who had acknowledged defeat despite winning against him at Desolate City. A rising disciple who came closer to the words *fair and righteous* than anyone Yeon-shin had met.

Had Jin Ju-rim extended his energy sense? A stream of inner power -- very subtle, almost courteous in its restraint -- scanned Yeon-shin's entire body.

"White Qilin and Lightning Genius," Jin Ju-rim said. "Among the main fortress's many rising talents, both showed great prominence. But which would be sharper? I was curious too." He paused. "Now I think Hwa-sin would take you in two moves."

Yeon-shin's expression did not change. But something behind his ribs tightened.

"Must be because you're not at full power," Jin Ju-rim added, as if that made it better.

*Two moves.*

It was not talk to be ignored. A blue-ranked master with hundreds of battles behind him had felt the truth in their energy waves and spoken plainly.

"Thank you for the advice," Yeon-shin said.

"Just advice? I spoke facts." Jin Ju-rim's gaze lingered. "Aren't you involved in the Clear Night Valley incident? Since you have grudges with the Namgung family, your justification can't be said to be less than Hwa-sin's -- a man who wants to point his sword at his own blood. Resolving what one started is also a warrior's virtue. I wish you success."

He left. Yeon-shin watched him go.

*Life-and-death martial matches against the Namgung family. The Grand Administrator said competition would be fierce.*

Namgung Hwa-sin was a man who had left his family and devoted himself to Desolate Sect. He would almost certainly volunteer. And if he competed against Yeon-shin, he would be an extremely powerful opponent.

*Two moves ahead.* The words burned.

Yeon-shin bowed to his companions. Cheong Myeong stood with his sword sheathed, crooked smile in place. Baek Mi-ryeo waited beside him, watching.

"Thank you," Yeon-shin said.

"What thanks?" Cheong Myeong ruffled his hair. "Stories of troublesome in-laws are common. Tell us whenever they act bothersome."

He showed no sign of fearing prestigious martial families. It was a confidence that bordered on indifference -- the serenity of a man who knew exactly what his sword could do.

Baek Mi-ryeo extended her long fingers to Yeon-shin's solar plexus. She closed her eyes briefly, feeling the meridians, then opened them.

"Your true qi operation is remarkable. It went as you said -- it won't affect your recovery." She brushed a strand of hair from her face. "We can keep handling the Ma family like this. However..."

Cheong Myeong's gaze drifted to Jin Ju-rim's retreating back. "It won't be easy for Lightning Genius to receive the mission either. White Qilin..."

"If the General Administration Office decided to strike off the Namgung family's head through martial matches," Baek Mi-ryeo said, "it means they'll consider face. They'll send warriors matching the opponent's age. Among Namgung leaders at Lightning Genius's age, there's only the young master and his sister. Not many spots." Her eyes found Yeon-shin's. "White Qilin is the competition."

*I know.*

He thought of the spirit pills still untouched in his quarters. He thought of internal energy -- quantity, density, the fundamental architecture of power that separated those raised on spirit medicines from those raised on nothing.

*This time is different. No Demon-Destroying Palm. No Sword Song. I must win with my original martial arts alone.*

He thought while walking back with his three companions. The crescent moon hung above, silver-grey, scattering pale light across the silence. The commotion raised by Won-chang and the others had already disappeared, swallowed by the night.

Not a trace remained. The stillness seemed to speak.

*Not petty schemes. Something more.*

---

The very next day, an official notice from the General Administration Office went up throughout the main fortress.

The Ma family disturbance was forgotten instantly. The impact of the contents was that great.

Hyeon Won-chang read the notice aloud, standing before the central wall of Radiant Demon Wing's great training ground with the posture of a scholar examining examination results.

"Life-and-death martial matches between the main fortress and Namgung family, to be carried out on the Great Heat of July. Warriors of the seventeen powers under the Divine Sword Squad should consider carefully and volunteer." He paused. "If there are many volunteers, there will be competitions. Divided by age. One-on-one."

"As expected," Baek Mi-ryeo said.

"Smashing famous martial arts under heaven brings honour," Cheong Myeong said, smiling faintly. "No warrior would hesitate to make their name renowned. The mission's merits will be enormous. Competition is inevitable."

Baek Mi-ryeo's gaze fixed on Yeon-shin. Cheong Myeong's did too -- though his was wrapped in that ever-present crooked amusement.

"Lightning Genius. Cheong Myeong and I won't step forward. We aren't in urgent need of merits." Her voice was level. Direct. "Win and take the mission. Can you do it?"

He did not answer immediately. The consideration behind their decision was plain. They knew about his constitution. They were clearing his path.

*Is this just gratitude?*

A feeling hard to express. It tickled somewhere deep.

"It's hard to say for certain now," he said. "There's something I need to know first."

"What?"

"The Sect Lord is calling."

Silence.

He kept his voice as calm as possible. "A letter came from the General Administration Office. Two hours ago. The Sect Lord will bestow rewards for the Blood Flame Sect headquarters matter."

Their eyes widened -- Won-chang's especially. But Yeon-shin was already turning the words over in his mind, the words that had been burning there since dawn, the single line from the second letter he had read and burned as instructed.

The writer had been beyond expectation.

Desolate Sect's lord.

Her flowing script had been engraved in his mind like a brand.

*I bestow a small piece. Come to me when the full moon rises.*

That was all. One line.

The crescent moon had been in the sky last night. Fifteen days until the full moon.

*A hundred days,* his heart said.

---
